description:
You are acting as an observability engineer who has to set up a very small heartbeat-style metric collector and guarantee that it is executed regularly.  
    Your goal is to prepare everything **inside the user’s account** so that automated tests can later confirm the setup.

    Required final state of the workstation
    ---------------------------------------

    1. Directory  
       • /home/user/observability **must exist**.

    2. Shell script  
       • Path         : /home/user/observability/collect_metrics.sh  
       • Permissions  : executable by the owner (any variant of “chmod +x” is fine).  
       • Exact file content (including the final newline character):  
         ```
         #!/usr/bin/env bash
         echo "$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')|heartbeat" >> /home/user/observability/metrics.log
         ```  

    3. Cron entry  
       • The user’s crontab (the one returned by the command that edits the
         current user’s crontab) **must contain one and only one new line** that reads  
         ```
         */5 * * * * /home/user/observability/collect_metrics.sh
         ```  
       • Do not add trailing spaces or comments on that line.

    4. Log file produced by the script  
       • Path         : /home/user/observability/metrics.log  
       • After you finish the task the file **must already contain exactly one line** because you will execute the script manually once.  
       • The single line has the format  
         `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS|heartbeat`  
         (24-hour clock, leading zeros, ISO date, no extra spaces).  
       • Example of a valid line: `2025-04-03 09:05:41|heartbeat`

    5. Verification file  
       • Path         : /home/user/observability/cron_validation.log  
       • File must contain **exactly three lines** (no blank lines, no spaces) in the
         order shown below:  
         ```
         CRON_SETUP: COMPLETE
         SCRIPT_EXECUTION: SUCCESS
         TOTAL_LINES_IN_LOG: 1
         ```

    Summary of what you need to accomplish
    --------------------------------------
    • Create the directory.  
    • Create the script with the exact content shown.  
    • Make the script executable.  
    • Install the specified cron line in the user’s crontab.  
    • Run the script manually once so that metrics.log gains a single entry.  
    • Generate the verification file with the three mandatory lines shown.

    The automated checker will verify:
    • Existence of the directory.  
    • Exact byte-for-byte content of collect_metrics.sh.  
    • Executable bit on collect_metrics.sh.  
    • Presence of the single cron line in the user’s crontab and no duplicates.  
    • Presence of metrics.log with exactly one “|heartbeat” line that matches the stated format.  
    • Presence and exact content of cron_validation.log.  

    You do not have to wait five minutes for cron execution; only the manual execution is required right now.
truth:
EXPECTED_SCRIPT_PATH=/home/user/observability/collect_metrics.sh
EXPECTED_SCRIPT_CONTENT="#!/usr/bin/env bash\necho \"\$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')|heartbeat\" >> /home/user/observability/metrics.log\n"
EXPECTED_CRON_LINE="*/5 * * * * /home/user/observability/collect_metrics.sh"
EXPECTED_METRICS_LOG=/home/user/observability/metrics.log
EXPECTED_METRICS_LOG_LINES=1
EXPECTED_VALIDATION_FILE=/home/user/observability/cron_validation.log
EXPECTED_VALIDATION_CONTENT="CRON_SETUP: COMPLETE\nSCRIPT_EXECUTION: SUCCESS\nTOTAL_LINES_IN_LOG: 1\n"
